title: Ambiguous case conditions
text: The ambiguous case arises when you are given certain information about a triangle that could lead to two possible triangles, one triangle, or no triangle.

hintAnswer: More than one possible triangle can be formed
title: Meaning of ambiguous case
text: What is the primary characteristic of an ambiguous case when solving a triangle?
choices: Only one triangle can be formed
More than one possible triangle can be formed
The triangle must be a right triangle

title: Law of Sines application
text: The Law of Sines is typically used when you have a side and its opposite angle, or two angles and a side.

title: Congruence criteria
text: Which of these is generally not a congruence postulate or theorem for triangles?

title: SSA and ambiguity
text: When you are given two sides and a non-included angle (SSA), there might be multiple ways to construct the triangle.

title: Identifying SSA
text: What abbreviation describes the situation where two sides and a non-included angle are known?
